Tesla (TSLA) Q2 2026 deliveries jump 25% to 480,126, beating estimates
Tesla delivered 480,126 vehicles in the second quarter of 2026, up 25% from the same quarter a year ago and roughly 74,000 vehicles above Wall Street’s consensus estimate.
According to Tesla’s official production and delivery report, the company produced 451,758 vehicles and delivered 480,126 in Q2 2026.
“Other Models” — Model S, Model X, Cybertruck, and Semi — contributed 8,822 produced and 12,364 delivered.
BYD delivered 557,090 fully electric vehicles in Q2 2026, keeping the Chinese automaker ahead of Tesla in global battery-electric sales.
Tesla now sits roughly 77,000 units behind BYD, down from a gap of more than 220,000 units a year ago."},{"url":"https://dallasexpress.com/business-markets/tesla-smashes-q2-records-480k-deliveries-13-5-gwh-energy-storage/","title":"Tesla Q2 2026: 480K Deliveries, 13.5 GWh Storage","content":"Jul 2, 2026 — Tesla reported over 480000 vehicle deliveries and 13.5 GWh energy storage in Q2 2026, with major growth at Gigafactory Texas.
Tesla, Inc. on Thursday released its second-quarter 2026 production and deliveries figures, reporting more than 450,000 vehicles produced and over 480,000 delivered.
The company also deployed 13.5 gigawatt-hours of energy storage products during the period.
The numbers reflect continued scaling of manufacturing and demand for Tesla vehicles and energy solutions.
Tesla’s Gigafactory Texas in Austin serves as a major hub for vehicle and battery production, as The Dallas Express previously reported."},{"url":"https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/TSLA/8-k-tesla-inc-reports-material-event-72d527c29eb5.html","title":"Tesla posts Q2 2026 vehicle production and deliveries | TSLA 8-K Filing","content":"Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) details Q2 2026 deliveries and energy storage

Energy storage deployments 13.5 GWh Q2 2026 energy storage products"},{"url":"https://www.notateslaapp.com/news/4389/tesla-smashes-q2-2026-delivery-estimates","title":"Tesla Smashes Q2 2026 Delivery Estimates - Not a Tesla App","content":"Tesla has officially reported a blowout second quarter for 2026, delivering 480,126 vehicles globally and marking a return to year-over-year growth.
In the weeks leading up to the report, market analysts had compiled a conservative delivery consensus of 406,024 vehicles, with even the most bullish financial institutions capping their estimates around the 420,000 mark.
Tesla's final delivery figure of 480,126 vehicles surpassed the baseline consensus by 74,000 units.
The quarter-over-quarter recovery is equally impressive, reflecting a 34% jump from the 358,023 vehicles delivered in the first quarter of 2026.
